description: Internment camp for Japanese-Americans during World War II
description_wiki: The Gila River War Relocation Center was an American concentration camp in Arizona, one of several built by the War Relocation Authority (WRA) during the Second World War for the incarceration of Japanese Americans from the West Coast of the United States.

description: former program of the United States government
description_wiki: The United States (U.S.) Cadet Nurse Corps (CNC) for women was authorized by the U.S. Congress on 15 June 1943 and signed into law by president Franklin D. Roosevelt on 1 July.

description: capital city of Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: Madison is the capital of the U.S. state of Wisconsin.

surface_forms: my hometown is in Vacaville, California
description: city in Solano County, California, United States of America
description_wiki: Vacaville is a city in Solano County, California, United States.

surface_forms: a small mom and pop store in Martinez, California
description: city in and seat of Contra Costa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Martinez (Spanish: Martínez) is a city in and the county seat of Contra Costa County, California, United States, in the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: city in Contra Costa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Concord ( KON-kerd) is the most populous city in Contra Costa County, California, United States.

description: city in Solano County, California, United States
description_wiki: Suisun City ( soo-SOON; Patwin for "where the west wind blows") is a city in Solano County, California, United States.

description: city in Stanislaus County, California, United States
description_wiki: Turlock is a city in Stanislaus County, California, United States.

description: detainee camp in Arizona, United States
description_wiki:
The Poston Internment Camp, located in Yuma County (now in La Paz County) in southwestern Arizona, was the largest (in terms of area) of the 10 American concentration camps operated by the War Relocation Authority during World War II.
The site was composed of three separate camps arranged in a chain from north to south, three miles from each other.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Arizona is a landlocked state in the Southwestern United States, sharing the Four Corners region with Colorado, New Mexico, and Utah.

The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Berkeley (  BURK-lee) is a city on the eastern shore of San Francisco Bay in northern Alameda County, California, United States.

The Japanese American Citizens League (日系アメリカ人市民同盟, Nikkei Amerikajin Shimin Dōmei) is an Asian American civil rights charity, headquartered in San Francisco, with regional chapters across the United States.

Nisei (二世, "second generation") is a Japanese-language term used in countries in North America and South America to specify the ethnically Japanese children born in the new country to Japanese-born immigrants, or Issei.

aliases: World War II Japanese American Internment Museum
